title: What's Next — Research Frontiers
description: Surveys the active research frontiers in real-time and offline rendering: neural rendering (NeRF, Gaussian Splatting for real-time novel view synthesis), real-time path tracing pipelines targeting 60 FPS with ReSTIR and next-generation hardware, photon mapping and light field capture for indoor rendering, and large-scale neural material inference. These are the technologies that will define the next generation of game rendering and represent the areas where curriculum-completing students are positioned to contribute professionally. Students will select one research area, read two foundational papers, and present a 15-minute technical summary with implementation feasibility analysis to a peer cohort.
